channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Columbia's Mahmoud Khalil felt he was being kidnapped as detention unfolded, lawyers say
item.description: Lawyers for Mahmoud Khalil say the detained Columbia University student felt he was being kidnapped after his arrest and swift transfer to Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dr. Mehmet Oz heads to the Senate with pitch to oversee America's health insurance programs
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, will testify before the Senate Finance Committee on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate works to avert partial government shutdown ahead of midnight deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate finds itself on Friday in a familiar position, working to avoid a partial government shutdown with just hours to spare as Democrats confront two painful options: allowing passage of a bill they believe gives President Donald Trump vast discretion on spending decisions or
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended. Here's what borrowers should know
item.description: The Trump administration’s recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Cora Lewis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two judges in mass firings cases order Trump administration to rehire probationary workers for now
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Two federal judges handed down orders requiring President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies Thursday, slowing down for now the president's dramatic downsizing of th
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, BRIAN WITTE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US transfers immigrants out of Guantanamo Bay to Louisiana as court weighs legality
item.description: Authorities have removed U.S. immigrants from detention facilities at Guantanamo Bay as a federal court in Washington weighs a challenge by civil rights advocates to holding immigrants at the offshore military station.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate Democrats signal plans to advance GOP bill to avoid a government shutdown
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-NY, said he plans to vote for a Republican bill to fund the government through September, paving the way for other Democrats to join him.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Hibbing manufacturer responds to Trump‘s steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: Canada is the biggest supplier of steel and aluminum for the U.S. The metals are used for products like nuts and bolts, sheet metal, machining and beer cans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Barely: House GOP passes government funding bill without help from Democrats
item.description: House Republicans this week passed a government funding bill 217-213 without assistance from Democrats, losing only one vote from Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Schumer says he will vote to advance GOP spending bill, lowering threat of shutdown
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) announced on the Senate floor Thursday that he plans to vote to advance a House-passed six-month government funding bill, despite the strong opposition of many Democrats who say the bill would create a “slush fund” for President Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Dems Divided
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems, facing shutdown, previously criticized Republicans for 'political games' during gov funding fights
item.description: Democrats have criticized Republicans for their hesitation to pass government funding legislation, while their own party is currently on the brink of allowing a federal shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Direct aid to Israel should be phased out to 'reduce US leverage,' influential conservative groups argue
item.description: The Heritage Foundation is responding to backlash against a report that suggested the U.S. should phase out direct aid to Israel so that a future president could not cut it off at will.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens retaliatory 200% tariff on European wine after EU proposes American whiskey tax
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened a 200% tariff on European wine, Champagne and spirits if the European Union goes forward with a planned tariff on American whiskey. The European import tax, which was unveiled in response to steel and aluminum tariffs by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California bill that would make it illegal to defend yourself pulled
item.description: Assembly Bill 1333, which would have changed what qualifies as self-defense and eliminated circumstances such as defending your home or property, has been pulled due to public pressure and criticism from District Attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signals cautious optimism on Russia accepting Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Thursday said he hoped Russia would “do the right thing” as his administration presses for a ceasefire agreement between Moscow and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: EU announces a $5 billion investment in South Africa as the tariffs war with Trump escalates
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — European Union leaders announced 4.7 billion euros ($5.1 billion) in investments in South Africa on Thursday to support green energy and vaccine production, and agreed to start talks on new trade deals with Africa's most advanced economy.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
